groups.title: NOTES ON SOCIAL PROGRAMME
groups.desc:
Questions 1-4
Complete the notes below. 
Writing NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect:
<divider/>
Example                                                                  Answer
Number of trips per month                                      <iu>5</iu>
<divider/>
Visit places which have:
                                 • historical interest
                                 • good $21792$
                                 • $21793$
Cost:                         between £5.00 and £15.00 per person
Note:                         special trips organised for groups of $21794$ people
Time:                         departure – 8.30 a.m
                                  return – 6.00 p.m.
To reserve a seat:     sign name on the $21795$ 3 days in advance

questions.analysis: 答案是“shopping”，因为在听力原文中提到学生们对购物的兴趣，并且“shopping”符合题干中“good”修饰的语境。题干中的定位词是“good”，在原文中对应的信号词是“variety of”，这表明提供多样化的购物选择是学生们感兴趣的内容。

答案是“guided tours”，因为听力原文中明确提到“we go for ones where we know there are guided tours, because this gives a good focus for the visit”。通过定位词“places”和信号词“places of historical interest”，我们可以准确找到答案所在的句子，并确定答案是“guided tours”。学生在听力过程中应特别注意这些关键词和信号词，以便准确捕捉答案。

正确答案是“more than 12”，因为听力原文明确提到“we do offer to arrange special trips if, you know, there are more than twelve people”。题干中的定位词“special trips”和“groups of people”在原文中有对应的信号词“special trips”和“more than twelve people”。学生需要注意这些定位词，并在听力过程中关注相关的信号词和数字。理解并识别“more than”这个短语是解答这道题的关键。
答案是“notice board”，因为听力原文明确提到“sign your name on the notice board”，这与题干中的“sign name on the 【    】 3 days in advance”直接对应。通过抓住关键词和名词短语，学生可以准确定位并填写正确答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 5-10
Complete the table given below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

content: Read the $21800$ or see Social Assistant: Jane $21801$

questions.analysis: 第5题的正确答案是“13 February”，因为听力原文明确提到第一个活动是在“thirteenth of February”。学生需要注意听力中的信号词“the first one”，并抓住日期表达的关键词。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过关注数字和日期来提高正确率。
答案是“Tower of London”，因为听力原文明确提到这是第二个事件的“optional extra”。通过识别定位词“Event 2”和信号词“London trip”，可以迅速定位到相关信息。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过关注关键词和记笔记来提高答题准确率。
答案是“Bristol”，因为在听力材料中，提到“After that there's Bristol on the third of March.”。学生需要关注“After that”这个信号词，并识别出地名“Bristol”作为答案。通过理解定位词和信号词的概念，学生可以更有效地定位到正确答案。
第8题的正确答案是“American Museum”。在听力内容中，当提到“Bath”和“twenty-third of March”时，考生应注意接下来的内容。听力中明确提到“optional visit”是“American Museum”，因此这是正确答案。考生需要理解“optional visit”是额外的参观地点，避免误填其他信息。
第9题的正确答案是“student newspaper”。在听力原文中，提到“By the way, if you want more information about any of the trips, have a look in the student newspaper.”，这句话明确指出了获取更多信息的途径是查看学生报纸。因此，答案是“student newspaper”。
第10题的正确答案是“Yentob”，因为在听力材料中，提到如果需要更多信息，可以联系助理Jane Yentob，并且拼写了她的名字。学生需要注意听力中的拼写部分，并且理解表格中需要填入的是人名而不是地点或其他信息。

transcript.en: MAN:Good morning.
transcript.cn: 男：早上好。

transcript.en: WOMAN:Good morning.
transcript.cn: 女：早上好。

transcript.en: How can I help you?
transcript.cn: 有什么可以帮到您？

transcript.en: MAN:I understand that the school organises...
transcript.cn: 男：我了解到学校有组织

transcript.en: umm, trips to different...
transcript.cn: 去不同的地方旅行...

transcript.en: WOMAN:Yes, we run five every month: three during weekends and two Wednesday afternoon trips.
transcript.cn: 女：是的，我们每月共有五次，3次周末出发，2次周三下午出发。

transcript.en: MAN:What sort of places?
transcript.cn: 男：都去些什么地方呢？

transcript.en: WOMAN:Well , obviously it varies , but always places of historical interest and also which offer a variety of shopping , because our students always ask about that...
transcript.cn: 女：嗯，很多地方，但是都基本上是历史古迹。还有一系列购物点，因为学生总是咨询这些信息。

transcript.en: and then we go for ones where we know there are guided tours , because this gives a good focus for the visit.
transcript.cn: 我们会去有导游解说的地点，因为这样更有针对性。

transcript.en: MAN:Do you travel far?
transcript.cn: 男：去的地方远吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN:Well, we're lucky here, obviously, because we're able to say that all our visits are less than three hours drive.
transcript.cn: 女：嗯，我们显然是比较幸运的，可以这么说吧，我们的旅行团车程少于3小时。

transcript.en: MAN:How much do they cost?
transcript.cn: 男：价格多少呢？

transcript.en: WOMAN:Again it varies - between five and fifteen pounds a head, depending on distance.
transcript.cn: 女：这要看具体旅游团，根据距离的差异，每人五到十五镑之间。

transcript.en: MAN:Ah ha ...
transcript.cn: 男：啊...这样。

transcript.en: WOMAN:Oh, and we do offer to arrange special trips if, you know, there are more than twelve people.
transcript.cn: 女人：哦，还有如果你们超过12人，我们可以提供定制旅行团。

transcript.en: MAN:Oh right.
transcript.cn: 男：那好。

transcript.en: I'll keep that in mind.
transcript.cn: 我记住了。

transcript.en: And what are the times normally?
transcript.cn: 通常什么时间出发呢？

transcript.en: WOMAN:We try to keep it pretty fixed so that, that students get to know the pattern.
transcript.cn: 女：我们尽量固定时间，好让学生知道我们的出团模式。

transcript.en: We leave at eight-thirty a. m. and return at six p. m.p.
transcript.cn: 我们早上八点半出发，下午六点回程。

transcript.en: We figure it's best to keep the day fairly short.
transcript.cn: 我们觉得最好还是短途的。

transcript.en: MAN:Oh yes.
transcript.cn: 男：哦，对，

transcript.en: And how do we reserve a place?
transcript.cn: 那我们怎么预订呢？

transcript.en: WOMAN:You sign your name on the notice board.
transcript.cn: 女：在公告栏上签名就好。

transcript.en: Do you know where it is?
transcript.cn: 你知道在哪里吗？

transcript.en: MAN:Ah ha.
transcript.cn: 男：嗯，

transcript.en: I saw it this morning.
transcript.cn: 今天早上我看见了。

transcript.en: WOMAN:And we do ask that you sign up three days in advance so we know we've got enough people interested to run it, and we can cancel if necessary, with full refund of course.
transcript.cn: 女：我们要求提前三天签名，这样我们就能统计感兴趣的人数了。如果有必要我们会取消发团，当然报名费会全额退款。

transcript.en: MAN:That's fine, thanks.
transcript.cn: 男：那好，谢谢。

transcript.en: And what visits are planned for this term?
transcript.cn: 这个学期有什么出团安排呢？

transcript.en: WOMAN:Right, well I'm afraid the schedule hasn't been printed out yet, but we have confirmed the dates and planned the optional extra visits which you can also book in advance if you want to.
transcript.cn: 女：对，我估计这个学期的计划表还没打印出来。但是我们已经确认好出团日期以及计划好额外的旅行团，如果你想提前预定也可以。

transcript.en: MAN:Oh that's all right.
transcript.cn: 男：哦，那可以。

transcript.en: If you can just give some idea of the weekend ones so I can, you know, work out when to see friends, etcetera.
transcript.cn: 如果你能先给我周末的出团信息，这样我就好安排什么时候见朋友等等。

transcript.en: WOMAN:Oh sure.
transcript.cn: 女：哦，没问题。

transcript.en: Well, the first one is St Ives.
transcript.cn: 第一个是圣艾夫斯。

transcript.en: That's on the thirteenth of February and we'll have only sixteen places available because we're going by minibus.
transcript.cn: 是在二月十三日去，我们只有16空位，因为我们是坐小巴去。

transcript.en: And that's a day in town with the optional extra of visiting the Hepworth Museum.
transcript.cn: 这个团是小镇一日游，可额外选择参观海浦沃斯博物馆。

transcript.en: MAN:Oh right...
transcript.cn: 男：哦，好的…

transcript.en: yeah...
transcript.cn: 好…

transcript.en: that sounds good.
transcript.cn: 听起来不错。

transcript.en: WOMAN:Then there's a London trip on the sixteenth of February and we'll be taking a medium-sized coach so there'll be forty-five places on that, and, let's see, the optional extra is the Tower of London.
transcript.cn: 女：然后2月16号还有一个伦敦游团，我们坐中型客车，所以那上面有45个位置。让我看看，这个团可额外选择参观伦敦塔。

transcript.en: MAN:Oh, I've already been there.
transcript.cn: 男：哦，我已经去过那里了。

transcript.en: WOMAN:After that there's Brisiol on the third of March.
transcript.cn: 女：呃，这个团之后是3月3号布里斯托团。

transcript.en: MAN:Where?
transcript.cn: 男：哪儿？

transcript.en: WOMAN:Bristol...
transcript.cn: 女：布里斯托…

transcript.en: B-R-I-S-T-O-L.
transcript.cn: B-R-I-S-T-O-L.

transcript.en: MAN:OK..
transcript.cn: 男：好的…

transcript.en: WOMAN:That's in a different minibus with eighteen places available , oh , and the optional extra is a visit to the S. S. Great Britain.
transcript.cn: 女：那是乘坐有18个座位的另一种小巴。可额外选择参观大不列颠号。

transcript.en: MAN:OK...
transcript.cn: 男：好吧。

transcript.en: WOMAN:We're going to Salisbury on the eighteenth of March and that's always a popular one because the optional extra is Stonehenge, so we're taking the large coach with fifty seats...
transcript.cn: 女：3月18号是索尔兹伯里的团，这个团很受欢迎，因为可以额外选择参观巨石阵。所以我们会乘坐50人的大巴车。

transcript.en: MAN:Oh good.
transcript.cn: 男：哦，很好啊。

transcript.en: WOMAN:And then the last one is to Bath on the twenty-third of March.
transcript.cn: 女：最后一个是3月23号去巴斯。

transcript.en: MAN:Oh yes.
transcript.cn: 男人：是吗，

transcript.en: Is Bath the Roman city?
transcript.cn: 巴斯是那个罗马城市吗？

transcript.en: WOMAN:Yes.
transcript.cn: 女：对啊。

transcript.en: that's right, and that's in the sixteen-seater minibus.
transcript.cn: 这个会坐16座的小巴。

transcript.en: MAN:And where's the optional visit?
transcript.cn: 男：额外景点是？

transcript.en: WOMAN:It's to the American Museum - well worth a visit.
transcript.cn: 女：是去美国博物馆，那是个很值得去的景点。

transcript.en: MAN:OK.
transcript.cn: 男：好的。

transcript.en: well that's great, thanks for all that...
transcript.cn: 真好，谢谢你啊。

transcript.en: WOMAN:My pleasure.
transcript.cn: 女：不客气。

transcript.en: By the way, if you want more information about any of the trips, have a look in the student newspaper.
transcript.cn: 顺便说下，如果想了解更多旅行团信息，你可以浏览学生报。

transcript.en: MAN:OK.
transcript.cn: 男：好的。

transcript.en: WOMAN:Or, have a word with my assistant;
transcript.cn: 女：或者可以问下我助手，

transcript.en: her name is Jane Yentob - that's Y-E-N-T-O-B.
transcript.cn: 她叫简·严拓，就是YENTOB。

transcript.en: MAN:Right, I've got that.
transcript.cn: 男：好，我知道了。

transcript.en: Thank you very much for all your help.
transcript.cn: 非常感谢您的帮助。

transcript.en: WOMAN:You're very welcome.
transcript.cn: 女：不客气。

transcript.en: I hope you enjoy the trips.
transcript.cn: 希望你旅途愉快。

groups.title: RIVERSIDE INDUSTRIAL VILLAGE
groups.desc:
Questions 11-13
Complete the sentences below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect:
Riverside Village was a good place to start an industry because it had water, raw materials and fuels such as $21802$ and $21802$.
The metal industry was established at Riverside Village by $21803$ who lived in the area.
There were over $21804$ water-powered mills in the area in the eighteenth century.

questions.analysis: 答案是“coal and firewood”，因为听力原文明确提到这些是当地丰富的燃料，使得Riverside Village成为一个适合工业发展的地方。考生需要通过定位词和信号词来快速找到答案所在的句子，并准确提取出答案词。

答案是“local craftsmen”，因为题干问的是谁在Riverside Village建立了金属工业。听力原文中提到“local craftsmen first built an iron forge”，这表明是当地工匠建立了金属工业。题干中的“metal industry”对应原文中的“iron forge”，“Riverside Village”对应“village”，“lived in the area”对应“local”。因此，正确答案是“local craftsmen”。
正确答案是“160”，因为听力原文明确提到“more than a hundred and sixty water mills”。题干中的定位词“water-powered mills”和“eighteenth century”帮助我们在听力过程中迅速锁定相关信息，并通过信号词“water mills”和“eighteenth centuries”确认答案位置。

groups.desc:
Questions 14-20
Complete the plan below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.analysis: 题目中的定位词是“Road”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“running along the bottom”。听力原文明确指出地图底部的道路名称是“Woodside Road”，因此答案是“Woodside Road”。学生需要注意完整填写路名，不能只填“Woodside”或“Road”。

第15题的正确答案是“Ticket Office”。在听力原文中，讲解员提到“Now we're standing at the entrance, see it at the bottom, and immediately to our right is the Ticket Office.”，这句话明确指出了售票处的位置就在入口的右边。通过定位词“Entrance”和信号词“immediately to our right”，可以准确找到答案。
第16题的正确答案是“Gift Shop”。通过定位词“Entrance”和信号词“by the entry gate”，可以准确找到听力原文中的对应句子，并从中提取出正确答案。学生在听力过程中应特别注意方位词和建筑物名称，以便准确填空。
答案是“Workshop”，因为听力原文明确指出“the big, stone building at the top”是“the main Workshop”。通过定位词“the 17”以及信号词“big, stone building at the top”，可以准确找到对应的建筑物，并确定其名称为“Workshop”。
答案是“showroom”，因为听力原文明确提到“top right-hand corner”位置的建筑是“Showroom”。学生在听力过程中应特别关注定位词和信号词，结合图示的位置，准确填写答案。
第19题的正确答案是“cafe”。在听力原文中，提到Grinding Shop时，紧接着提到“on one side of that”，这提示了答案即将出现。然后提到在Grinding Shop的一侧是Engine Room，另一侧是Cafe。因此，Cafe是正确答案。
答案是“cottages”，因为听力原文明确提到“the cottages”是“built for the workers”，这与题干中的“for the workers”相对应。通过定位词“for the workers”可以快速找到相关信息，并且原文中直接给出了答案。

transcript.en: Good afternoon everybody and welcome to Riverside Industrial Village.
transcript.cn: 大家下午好，欢迎来到滨江工业村。

transcript.en: To start your visit, I'm just going to give you a brief account of the history of the museum before letting you roam about on your own.
transcript.cn: 参观开始前，首先容我在你们自由游览前简短介绍下博物馆的历史。

transcript.en: I won't keep you long.
transcript.cn: 不会耽误大家很长时间，

transcript.en: OK?
transcript.cn: 可以吗？

transcript.en: Now, from where we're standing you've got a good view of the river over there.
transcript.cn: 现在我们所站的地方，你们可以将对岸河景尽收眼底。

transcript.en: And it was because of this fast-flowing water that this site was a natural place for manufacturing works.
transcript.cn: 因为水流急，这里是生产工作的天然地点

transcript.en: The water and the availability of raw materials in the area, like minerals and iron ore, and also the abundance of local fuels, like coal and firewood, all made this site suitable for industry from a very early time.
transcript.cn: 水，还有这个区域的原材料例如矿物和铁矿，还有丰富的本地燃料，例如煤和炭。这些都使这里成为早期工业的合适地点。

transcript.en: Water was the main source of power for the early industries and some of the water wheels were first established in the twelfth century, would you believe?
transcript.cn: 早期工业主要靠水力提供能源，你信吗？早在12世纪这里就有水磨了。

transcript.en: At that time, local craftsmen first built an iron forge just behind the village here, on the bend in the river.
transcript.cn: 那个时候，本地工匠在村后河的转弯处首先建了一个炼铁炉。

transcript.en: By the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries the region's rivers supported more than a hundred and sixty water mills - and many of these continued to operate well into the nineteenth century.
transcript.cn: 到17和18世纪，这带河流支持超过160个水磨，直到19世纪，其中有很多还运作良好。

transcript.en: But then the steam engine was invented and then the railways came and the centres of industry were able to move away from the rivers and the countryside and into the towns.
transcript.cn: 但是后来发明了蒸汽机，随后火车的引入，工业的中心从河边和郊区迁移到镇上，

transcript.en: So, industrial villages like this one became very rare.
transcript.cn: 所以像这样的工业村变得很罕见。

transcript.en: So that's the history for you.
transcript.cn: 这就是它的历史了。

transcript.en: If you'd like any more information, you can ask me some questions, or you can read further in our excellent guide book.
transcript.cn: 如果你想要更多信息，可以提问或者阅读下我们很棒的游览手册。

transcript.en: Now I'm going to give you a plan of the site and I'd just like to point out where everything is and then you can take a look at everything for yourself.
transcript.cn: 现在我给大家介绍下分布图，告诉大家具体位置，然后你们可以自行参观。

transcript.en: I've already pointed out the river, which is on the left.
transcript.cn: 我之前已经指出河在左边。

transcript.en: And of course, running along the bottom is Woodside Road, got it?
transcript.cn: 当然沿河到底是Woodside路。明白吗？

transcript.en: OK.
transcript.cn: 好，

transcript.en: Now we're standing at the entrance, see it at the bottom, and immediately to our right is the Ticket Office.
transcript.cn: 现在我们所在的位置是入口。看，在图的最底下。右手边就是售票处。

transcript.en: You won't need that because you've got your group booking, but just past it are the toilets - always good to know where they are.
transcript.cn: 你们是团购，所以不需理会售票处。过了售票处就是厕所，提前知道总是好的。

transcript.en: In front of us is the car park, as you can see, and to the left, by the entry gate is the Gift Shop.
transcript.cn: 在我们前方是停车场，它左边就是礼品店的入口。

transcript.en: That's where you can get copies of the guide, like this one here.
transcript.cn: 在那可以买到像这本一样的引导手册。

transcript.en: Now, beyond the car park all the buildings are arranged in a half circle with a yard in the middle.
transcript.cn: 现在看到停车场前面，所有的建筑物都是以中间的庭院为中心，呈半圆型分布。

transcript.en: The big, stone building at the top is the main Workshop.
transcript.cn: 最顶的那个大石头建筑就是主要的作坊。

transcript.en: That's where the furnace is and where all the metal was smelted and the tools were cast, as you'll be able to see.
transcript.cn: 炼铁炉就在这里，所有的金属在这熔炼，工具也在这铸造，后面你可以看到。

transcript.en: Now, in the top right-hand corner, that building with bigger windows is the Showroom, where samples of all the tools that were made through the ages are on display.
transcript.cn: 现在看到右上角，那个窗户稍大的建筑就是展示厅。那个时期所锻造的工具样本都在那展出。

transcript.en: In the top left corner is the Grinding Shop, where the tools were sharpened and finished.
transcript.cn: 在左上角是打磨作坊，所有工具在那进行磨尖和完成。

transcript.en: And on one side of that you can see the Engine Room and on the other is the Cafe, which isn't an antique, you'll be pleased to know, though they do serve very nice old-fashioned teas.
transcript.cn: 在打磨作坊的一边是引擎房，另一边就是咖啡厅，咖啡厅不是古董，知道这点你们应该会很开心，尽管咖啡厅供应传统茶饮。

transcript.en: The row of buildings you can see on the left are the cottages.
transcript.cn: 在左边那排建筑是小木屋。

transcript.en: These were built for the workers towards the end of the eighteenth century and they're still furnished from that period so you can get a good idea of ordinary people's living conditions.
transcript.cn: 这些小木屋在18世纪末为工匠们建造，现在依然沿用旧时的布置，所以你可以很一目了然的知道当时人们的居住环境。

transcript.en: Across the yard from them, you can see the stables where the horses were kept for transporting the products.
transcript.cn: 穿过庭院就是马厩。负责运送货物的马匹都饲养在这。

transcript.en: And the separate building in front of them is the Works Office and that still has some of the old accounts on display.
transcript.cn: 马厩前的独立建筑是事务办公室。这里依然有一些老账户本展出。

transcript.en: Right, if anyone wants a guided tour then I'm starting at the Engine Room.
transcript.cn: 好了，如果有人需要导游陪同参观，我会从引擎房开始讲解。

transcript.en: If you'd like to come along, this way please, ladies and gentlemen.
transcript.cn: 请跟我来，女士们先生们。

groups.desc: Questions 21 and 22 Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Example 　　Melanie could not borrow any books from the library because 　　Ⓐ the library was out. 　　Ⓑ she didn't have time to look. 　　🅒 the books had already been borrowed.

questions.title: Melanie says she has not started the assignment because

options.title: A
options.content: she was doing work for another course.

options.title: B
options.content: it was a really big assignment.

options.title: C
options.content: she hasn’t spent time in the library.

questions.analysis: Melanie没有开始第二个作业的原因是她在做另一个课程的作业。她明确表示她把所有时间都花在另一个作业上，这就是她没有开始第二个作业的原因。因此，选项A是正确的。

questions.title: The lecturer says that reasonable excuses for extensions are
questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为听力原文明确提到“extensions are normally given only for medical or compassionate reasons”，这与选项C中的“personal illness or accident”相符。学生在听力过程中应特别注意定位词和信号词，以便快速定位到相关信息并做出正确选择。

groups.desc:
Questions 23-27
What recommendations does Dr Johnson make about the journal articles?
Choose your answers from the box and write the letters A-G next to questions 23–27.

questions.title: Jackson
questions.analysis: 正确答案是E. read research methods。Dr Johnson明确指出，关于Jackson的文章，学生只需要看研究方法部分。这一信息在听力原文中通过“research methodology”这一关键词传达出来。通过抓住定位词“Jackson”和信号词“research methodology”，学生可以准确找到答案。

questions.title: Roberts
questions.analysis: Dr Johnson 对 Roberts 文章的评价是“very relevant”，Melanie 也确认了这一点，表示“if it's useful, I'll try and read that one”。因此，正确答案是“B. useful”。

questions.title: Morris
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是G. don’t read。通过定位词“Morris”和信号词“Now, the one by Morris”，可以迅速找到相关的听力内容。听到“wouldn't bother”这样的否定词，可以确定Dr Johnson不推荐阅读这篇文章。学生在听力过程中应特别注意这些关键的否定词，以避免误选。

questions.title: Cooper
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是F. read conclusion。Dr Johnson明确指出，关于Cooper的文章，学生只需要看最后部分，即研究结果部分。这一信息在听力原文中通过“last part”和“research results”这两个关键词传达出来。通过抓住定位词“Cooper”和信号词“last part”，学生可以准确定位到答案。

questions.title: Forster
questions.analysis: Dr. Johnson 对 Forster 文章的评价是“It's not bad and could be of some help, but not that much”，意思是这篇文章不算差，可能有些帮助，但帮助不大。这表明这篇文章的价值有限，因此正确答案是 C. limited value。

groups.desc:
Questions 28-30
Label the chart below.
Choose your answers from the box below and write the letters A-H next to questions 28–30.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是“D. noisy neighbours”，因为听力原文中提到的“Bar two”与“people living nearby disturbing them”直接对应。通过定位词“28”和信号词“Bar two”，学生可以迅速找到相关信息，并通过关键词“disturbing”确定答案。

在听力材料中，Dr. Johnson 详细解释了每个柱状图的含义。对于第29题，对应的是“bar number four”，Dr. Johnson 解释说：“bar number four refers to problems when the owner of the property won’t help fix things that go wrong. In other words, the owner is not helpful and so the tenants move out.” 这句话的意思是，第四个柱状图表示的是房东不合作，不愿意修理房子的问题，导致租户搬走。因此，正确答案是 A. uncooperative landlord。
本题的正确答案是“B. environment”，因为听力原文中提到的“more attractive neighbourhood”直接对应了“environment”。通过定位词“30”和信号词“bar number six”，学生可以迅速找到答案所在的部分，并通过关键词“attractive neighbourhood”确定答案。

transcript.en: MELANIE:Excuse me, DR JOHNSON.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：您好，约翰逊博士。

transcript.en: May I speak to you for a minute?
transcript.cn: 我能跟您聊一会吗？

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Sure.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：当然。

transcript.en: Come in.
transcript.cn: 请进。

transcript.en: MELANIE:I'm MELANIE Griffin.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：我是梅兰妮·格里芬。

transcript.en: I'm taking your course in Population Studies.
transcript.cn: 我是您人口研究课的学生。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Right.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：好的。

transcript.en: Well, MELANIE, how can I help you?
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮，那我能帮你什么呢？

transcript.en: MELANIE:I'm...
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：我..

transcript.en: having a bit of trouble with the second assignment, and it's due in twelve days.
transcript.cn: 第二个作业我遇到点问题，还有12天就要交了

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:What sort of trouble are you having?
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：什么问题呢？

transcript.en: Is the assignment question a problem?
transcript.cn: 是作业题目吗？

transcript.en: MELANIE:Well, that's part of the problem.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：嗯，其中一部分吧。

transcript.en: I'm also having - been having - trouble getting hold of the books.
transcript.cn: 我有，其实一直有个问题是找不到需要的书。

transcript.en: I've been to the library several times, and all the books are out.
transcript.cn: 我已经去过几次图书馆了，所有书都借出了。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Sounds like you should have started borrowing books a bit earlier.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：听起来你该早点开始借书啊。

transcript.en: MELANIE:Well, I had  a really big assignment due in for another course, and I've been spending all my time on that, and I thought...
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：嗯，其实是我另外一门课有个大作业要交，我一直把时间都花在那上面，想着......

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:...You might get an extension of time to finish your assignment for me?
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：在我这，你可以延迟交作业的日期。

transcript.en: MELANIE:If that's possible, but I don't know ...
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：可能吗？但是，我不知道...

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Well, yes, it is possible, but extensions are normally given only for  medical or compassionate reasons, otherwise it's really a question of organising your study, and we don't like giving extensions to students who simply didn't plan their work properly.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：嗯是可能的，不过一般延迟日期只批给医疗和私事原因。否则这就是你组织学习的问题了，我们也不愿意让一些不会合理安排学习时间的学生延迟交作业。

transcript.en: What did you get for your first assignment?
transcript.cn: 那你上次的作业分数是多少？

transcript.en: MELANIE:I got eighty-seven percent.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：我拿了78分。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Mmm, yes, you did very well indeed, so obviously you can produce good work.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：嗯，成绩很好啊。很明显你的作业质量很高。

transcript.en: MELANIE:I don't think I'll need too much extra time, as long as I can get hold of some of the important references.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：我不需要延迟太久，只要我拿到一些重要的参考资料。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Well, since you did so well in your first assignment.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：鉴于你上次作业做得那么好，

transcript.en: I'm prepared to give you an extra two weeks for this one, so that'll mean you'll need to submit it about a month from now.
transcript.cn: 我就批你延迟2个星期交吧，也就是你得下个月交。

transcript.en: MELANIE:Thank you.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：太感谢了。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Now, what about the reading materials?
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：那你的阅读资料到底怎么了？

transcript.en: Have you checked out the journal articles in the list?
transcript.cn: 你查阅过单子上的期刊文章吗？

transcript.en: MELANIE:Umm, no, not yet, there were about twenty of them, and I wasn't sure which ones would be most useful or important.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：额，还没呢。有20个，我不是很清楚到底哪些最有用和最重要。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Well, they're all useful, but I don't expect anyone to read them all, because a number of them deal with the same issues.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：好吧，都很有用，但是我也不指望你们都读了，毕竟里面很多讲得是同一个问题。

transcript.en: Let me give you some suggestions.
transcript.cn: 让我给你提些建议吧。

transcript.en: The article by Anderson and Hawker is really worth reading.
transcript.cn: 安德森和霍克写的文章很值得一读。

transcript.en: MELANIE:Right.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：好的。

transcript.en: I'll read that one.
transcript.cn: 我会看那个的。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:You should also read the article by Jackson, but just look at the part on the  research methodology - how they did it.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：你还该看杰克逊写的那篇文章，但是只需看研究方法论，就是他们如何做的。

transcript.en: MELANIE:OK ...
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：好的…

transcript.en: Jackson, got that...
transcript.cn: 杰克逊，知道了…

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:And if you have time, the one by Roberts says very relevant things, although it's not essential.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：如果时间充裕的话，可以看看罗伯茨的文章，有相关的资料，但是不是必须的。

transcript.en: MELANIE:So, OK, if it's  useful, I'll try and read that one ...
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：那......如果有用的话我会读读看。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Now, the one by Morris.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：还有莫里斯的，

transcript.en: I wouldn't bother with that at this stage, if I were you.
transcript.cn: 我是你的话，现在阶段来说可以不要理会。

transcript.en: MELANIE:OK, I  won't bother with Morris.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：好的，忽略莫里斯的。

transcript.en: Oh, now, someone told me the article by Cooper is important.
transcript.cn: 噢，还有人告诉我库伯的文章也很重要。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Well, yes, in a way, but just look at  the last part, where he discusses the research results.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：嗯，某程度来说是。但是只要看最后讨论研究结果那一部分就好。

transcript.en: And lastly, there's Forster - I can't think why I included that one.
transcript.cn: 最后是福斯特。我都不知道为什么我要加上他的。

transcript.en: It's not bad and could be of some help, but not that much.
transcript.cn: 不错，有点用但是又不是很重要。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Now, let's deal with the assignment question.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：现在我们来看看作业的问题。

transcript.en: What's the problem there?
transcript.cn: 有什么问题啊？

transcript.en: MELANIE:It's the graph on page two.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：这是第二页的图。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:What seems to be the problem?
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：有什么问题呢？

transcript.en: It's just the bar graph showing reasons why people change where they live.
transcript.cn: 就是一副描述人们迁移住所原因的柱状图。

transcript.en: MELANIE:Well.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：好。

transcript.en: I've got a photocopy but the reasons at the bottom are missing.
transcript.cn: 我有复印件，但是底下的原因没印上。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Ah, OK.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：噢，这样！

transcript.en: Look at the first bar on the graph - now that indicates the number of people who move because they want more space.
transcript.cn: 先看看图中第一个柱形，说得是因为需要更多空间而搬的人数。

transcript.en: MELANIE:Oh I see ...
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：哦，我明白了…

transcript.en: bar one.
transcript.cn: 第一条。

transcript.en: OK ...
transcript.cn: 好…

transcript.en: Now what about the next bar?
transcript.cn: 那第二个柱形呢？

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Bar two is to do with  the people living nearby disturbing them, so they chose to move away to somewhere quieter.
transcript.cn: 第二个柱形说的是因为邻居扰民，为了搬去安静点的地方的人数。

transcript.en: Now let's look at bar number three ...
transcript.cn: 现在来看看第三个柱形，

transcript.en: another reason people change their place of living is because they want to be closer to the city.
transcript.cn: 说的是因为想住的近市区而搬的人数。

transcript.en: MELANIE:OK.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：好的。

transcript.en: Proximity to the city is an issue...
transcript.cn: 靠近市区是个问题。

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Now ...
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：现在…

transcript.en: bar number four refers to problems when the owner of the property won't help fix things that go wrong.
transcript.cn: 第四个柱形是因为房东不肯负责维修而搬迁的人数。

transcript.en: In other words, the owner is not helpful and so the tenants move out.
transcript.cn: 换句话说就是房东不给力，租客搬离。

transcript.en: MELANIE:OK ...
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：好的…

transcript.en: Now what about bar five?
transcript.cn: 那第五个呢？

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Bar five is about those people who move because they need a bus or train to get them into the city or to go to work.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：第五个柱形图指的是因为需要坐巴士或火车到市区上班的人数。

transcript.en: MELANIE:OK ...
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：好的…

transcript.en: And bar six?
transcript.cn: 那第六个呢？

transcript.en: DR JOHNSON:Bar number six is interesting.
transcript.cn: 约翰逊博士：第六个原因挺有意思，

transcript.en: That reason was given quite a lot - people moving because they wanted to be in a more attractive neighbourhood.
transcript.cn: 包含意思也很广，说的是，搬是因为想住在更好的小区。

transcript.en: MELANIE:Oh, yes, thank you very much.
transcript.cn: 梅兰妮：嗯，太谢谢您了。

groups.title: THE URBAN LANDSCAPE
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect: Two areas of focus: • the effect of vegetation on the urban climate • ways of planning our $21822$ better Large-scale impact of trees: • they can make cities more or less $21823$ • in summer they can make cities cooler • they can make inland cities more $21824$ Local impact of trees: • they can make local areas         –– more $21825$         –– cooler         –– more humid         –– less windy         –– less $21826$ Comparing trees and buildings Temperature regulation: • trees evaporate water through their $21827$ • building surfaces may reach high temperatures Wind force: • tall buildings cause more wind at $21828$ level • trees $21829$ the wind force Noise: • trees have a small effect on traffic noise • $21830$ frequency noise passes through trees Important points to consider: • trees require a lot of sunlight, water and $21831$ to grow

questions.analysis: 正确答案是“cities”，因为听力原文中明确提到“how we can better plan our cities using trees to provide a more comfortable environment for us to live in”。题干中的定位词是“planning”，而信号词是“plan”。即使学生基础较差，只要抓住这些关键词，并结合上下文理解，也能较为准确地填入正确答案。
答案是“windy”，因为听力材料中明确提到“make a city... less windy or more windy”。题干中的定位词是“cities”，在听力材料中对应的信号词是“city”。通过抓住关键描述“less windy or more windy”，可以确定答案是“windy”。
答案是“humid”，因为听力原文明确提到“they can make it a bit more humid if it's a dry inland city”。通过定位词“inland cities”和信号词“dry inland city”，可以准确找到答案所在的句子，并从中提取出正确的答案词“humid”。
答案是“shady”，因为在听力原文中明确提到“trees can make the local area more shady, cooler, more humid and much less windy”。题干要求填入的词是“more 【 】”，而“shady”是符合题干语法结构的形容词，表示“阴凉的”。其他选项如“cooler”和“humid”虽然也在列举中，但不符合题干的语法要求。

答案是“dangerous”，因为听力材料明确提到“trees and planting of various kinds can be used to make city streets actually less dangerous in particular areas”。考生需要通过定位词“local areas”找到对应的信号词“particular areas”，并在听到相关信息时，迅速记录答案。
答案是“leaves”，因为听力原文明确提到“trees evaporate water through its leaves”。学生需要在听力过程中抓住“evaporate water”这个定位词，并在听到这个短语后集中注意力，听出后面的“leaves”这个答案词。通过理解定位词和信号词的概念，学生可以更有效地在听力过程中找到正确答案。
本题的正确答案是“ground”，因为听力材料中明确提到“high buildings make it windier at ground level”。考生需要注意“tall buildings”和“high buildings”的同义关系，以及“windier”后面通常会提到具体的地点。通过抓住这些关键词和逻辑关系，即使基础较差的学生也能较为准确地找到答案。
答案是“reduce”，因为听力原文中明确提到“Trees filter the wind and considerably reduce it”，其中“reduce”直接对应题干中的“wind force”。通过定位词“trees”和“wind force”，以及信号词“filter the wind, reduce it”，可以准确找到答案。
答案是“low”，因为听力材料中明确提到“Low-frequency noise, in particular, just goes through the trees as though they aren't there.”。通过定位词“frequency noise”和信号词“low-frequency noise”，考生可以准确找到答案所在的句子，并填入正确的单词“low”。
题干中提到树木生长需要阳光和水，这些是定位词。在听力原文中，提到“Trees need water to grow. They also need some sunlight to grow and you need room to put them.”，其中“room”是指“空间”，与题干中的“space”同义。因此，正确答案是“room”。

transcript.en: Good day, ladies and gentlemen.
transcript.cn: 女士们，先生们，大家好。

transcript.en: I have been asked today to talk to you about the urban landscape.
transcript.cn: 今天的演讲是关于城市景观。

transcript.en: There are two major areas that I will focus on in my talk: how vegetation can have a significant effect on urban climate, and how we can better plan our cities using trees to provide a more comfortable environment for us to live in.
transcript.cn: 我的演讲主要针对两点：植被是如何对城市气候产生重大影响的。如何利用树木更好地规划更加舒适的城市居住环境。

transcript.en: Trees can have a significant impact on our cities.
transcript.cn: 树木对我们城市的影响是巨大的。

transcript.en: They can make a city, as a whole, a bit less windy or a bit more windy, if that's what you want.
transcript.cn: 他们能使城市成为一个整体，少些风或者需要的话，多些风。

transcript.en: They can make it a bit cooler if it's a hot summer day in an Australian city, or they can make it a bit more humid if it's a dry inland city.
transcript.cn: 如果是澳洲市区炎热的夏天，树木能使天气凉快一点，又或者如果是干燥的内陆城市，树木能使空气湿润些。

transcript.en: On the local scale - that is, in particular areas within the city - trees can make the local area more shady, cooler, more humid and much less windy.
transcript.cn: 在本地区域，尤其是城市内某些区域，树木能使该区域更多树荫，更凉快，更湿润，也同时大大减少风力。

transcript.en: In fact trees and planting of various kinds can be used to make city streets actually less dangerous in particular areas.
transcript.cn: 事实上，在市内特定的区域，树木和各类植被能使城市路面危险度降低一些。

transcript.en: How do trees do all that, you ask?
transcript.cn: 树木是如何做到这些的呢？

transcript.en: Well, the main difference between a tree and a building is a tree has got an internal mechanism to keep the temperature regulated.
transcript.cn: 嗯，树木和建筑最主要的区别是树木自身的内部机制能调节温度。

transcript.en: It evaporates water through its leaves and that means that the temperature of the leaves is never very far from our own body temperature.
transcript.cn: 水分通过树叶蒸发，这意味着树叶的温度不会和人类的体温相差太远。

transcript.en: The temperature of a building surface on a hot sunny day can easily be twenty degrees more than our temperature.
transcript.cn: 艳阳炙烤下的建筑物表面温度轻易就能超过体温20度。

transcript.en: Trees, on the other hand, remain cooler than buildings because they sweat.
transcript.cn: 而另一方面，树木能持续保持比建筑物温度低，因为他们能出汗。

transcript.en: This means that they can humidify the air and cool it - a property which can be exploited to improve the local climate.
transcript.cn: 这就意味这他们能湿润空气，然后降温，而这个特质能被用于改善本地气候。

transcript.en: Trees can also help break the force of winds.
transcript.cn: 树木还能帮助分解风力。

transcript.en: The reason that high buildings make it windier at ground level is that, as the wind goes higher and higher, it goes faster and faster.
transcript.cn: 之所以高楼的底层风力更大是因为随着风向增高，风速会越来越快。

transcript.en: When the wind hits the building, it has to go somewhere.
transcript.cn: 当风撞击到建筑物时，风必须吹向一个方向。

transcript.en: Some of it goes over the top and some goes around the sides of the building, forcing those high level winds down to ground level.
transcript.cn: 有些风走向建筑物顶端，而有些则围绕建筑物，这迫使高处的风走向建筑物底层。

transcript.en: That doesn't happen when you have trees.
transcript.cn: 当有树木的时候，这就不会发生。

transcript.en: Trees filter the wind and considerably reduce it, preventing those very large strong gusts that you so often find around tall buildings.
transcript.cn: 树木能过滤风并很大程度上减低风力，阻止形成高楼周围常见的强风。

transcript.en: Another problem in built-up areas is that traffic noise is intensified by tall buildings.
transcript.cn: 在高楼林立区域的另外一个问题是高楼会加强交通噪音。

transcript.en: By planting a belt of trees at the side of the road, you can make things a little quieter, but much of the vehicle noise still goes through the trees.
transcript.cn: 通过在路两旁种植树带能使马路安静些，但是大部分的噪音还是会穿树而过。

transcript.en: Trees can also help reduce the amount of noise in the surroundings, although the effect is not as large as people like to think.
transcript.cn: 树木还能减低周围环境的噪音，虽然不如我们想象中有效。

transcript.en: Low- frequency noise, in particular, just goes through the trees as though they aren't there.
transcript.cn: 特别是低频噪音无视树木的存在，直接穿过他们释放出来。

transcript.en: Although trees can significantly improve the local climate, they do however take up a lot of space.
transcript.cn: 虽说树木能大大改善本地气候，但确实也很占空间。

transcript.en: There are root systems to consider and branches blocking windows and so on.
transcript.cn: 例如根系，树枝挡着窗等。

transcript.en: It may therefore be difficult to fit trees into the local landscape.
transcript.cn: 所以将树放置在本地景观中可能有困难。

transcript.en: There is not a great deal you can do if you have what we call a street canyon - a whole set of high-rises enclosed in a narrow street.
transcript.cn: 如果碰到街道峡谷，就是高楼成排街道狭窄，那也就无能为力了。

transcript.en: Trees need water to grow.
transcript.cn: 树木生长需要水。

transcript.en: They also need some sunlight to grow and you need room to put them.
transcript.cn: 他们也需要一些阳光来成长，你需要空间来种植。

transcript.en: If you have the chance of knocking buildings down and replacing them, then suddenly you can start looking at different ways to design the streets and to introduce...
transcript.cn: 如果有机会推倒建筑再建，那时就可以开始用不同的方式审视街道设计了，引入......
